To be used in place of code^space^desc ! structure when OSS file pathname has ! been used in the code^range^file^name ! specification. Begin Int Code^space;! <0>=0 user, =1 system ``` Definitions of the CODE^SPACE^OSS^DESC fields that differ from the CODE^SPACE^DESC descriptor follow. For definitions of all other fields, see CODE^SPACE^DESC. CODE^RANGES^FILE^NAME is a structure that contains the internal-format file name of the OSS file pathname. pathid INT:value:12 is the internal representation of the OSS file pathname for code file. To indicate all PATHIDs, initialize PATHID[0] to -1. crvsn INT:value:3 is the creation version serial number that identifies a unique instance of an OSS disk file. To indicate all CRVSN values, initialize CRVSN[0] to -1. # SQLPROC^DESCSQLPROC^DESC is the descriptor for SQLPROC entities. For OSS file pathnames, use SQLPROC^OSS^DESC. type INT:value:1 is the literal SQLPROC^T or the numeric identifier 15. A positive type value includes the specified entities in the measurement configuration. A negative type value excludes the specified entities from measurement. For an example, see Specifying Entity Descriptors. len INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor. len is 40. cpu^number INT:value:1 ``` ! <1>=0 non lib, =1 lib ! <2>=0 non native, =1 native ! <7>=0 no OSS, =1 OSS MID included. ! <8:15> space number Int Code^ranges^file^name[0:11]; String Code^ranges^file^name^s = Code^ranges^file^name; Struct Code^ranges^file^name^MID;! Internal identifier for OSS pathname Begin Int Pathid[0:11];! Internal format OSS file pathname Int Crvsn[0:2];! Creation Volume Sequence Number End; End; ``` ``` Struct Sqlproc^desc (*); Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11] End; ``` is the number of the CPU on which the process to be measured is running. To indicate all CPUs, use the literal ‑1. pin INT:value:1 is the process identification number of the process to be measured. To indicate all PINs, use the literal ‑1. process^name INT:value:4 is the name of the process to be measured. The name must be left‑justified and blank‑filled. It must consist of a dollar sign ($) followed by a process name in internal format. To indicate all processes, use a dollar sign followed by an asterisk ($*). program^file^name INT:value:12 is the name of the object file that the process to be measured is executing. The name must be left‑justified, blank‑filled, and in local internal format. You can use asterisks (*) to specify the volume, subvolume, and file-name fields within program^file^name. # SQLPROC^OSS^DESCSQLPROC^OSS^DESC is the descriptor for SQLPROC entities that supports OSS file pathnames. Definitions of the SQLPROC^OSS^DESC fields that differ from the SQLPROC^DESC descriptor follow. For definitions of all other fields, see SQLPROC^DESC. len INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor. len is 40 if the descriptor does not contain the PROGRAM^FILENAME^MID structure. If the descriptor does contain the PROGRAM^FILENAME^MID structure, len is 70. PROGRAM^FILE^NAME^MID is a structure that contains the internal-format file name for the OSS pathname. pathid INT:value:12 is the internal format representation of an OSS file pathname for the specified program file name if the file is part of an OSS file set. To indicate all PATHIDs, initialize PATHID[0] to -1. crvsn INT:value:3 is the creation version serial number that identifies a unique instance of an OSS disk file. Initialize CRVSN[0] to -1 to indicate all CRVSN values. ``` Struct Sqlproc^OSS^desc (*); Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11]; Struct Program^file^name^MID;! Internal identifier for OSS pathname Begin Int Pathid[0:11];! Internal format OSS file pathname Int Crvsn[0:2];! Creation Volume Sequence Number End; End; ``` # SQLSTMT^DESCSQLSTMT^DESC is the descriptor for SQLSTMT entities. For OSS file pathnames, use SQLSTMT^OSS^DESC. For ANSI SQL file names, use SQLSTMT^ANSI^DESC. ``` NOTENOTE When the measurement is being configured, only the process part of the descriptor is used; when performing a listing, all the fields can be used. ``` type INT:value:1 is the literal SQLSTMT^T or the numeric identifier 16. A positive type value includes the specified entities in the measurement configuration. A negative type value excludes the specified entities from measurement. For an example, see Specifying Entity Descriptors. len INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor. len is 74. cpu^number INT:value:1 is the number of the CPU on which the process to be measured is running. pin INT:value:1 is the process identification number of the process to be measured. You can use these literal values: ‑1 to indicate all processes SYSPROCS to indicate all processes installed by SYSGEN process^name INT:value:4 is the name of the process to be measured. The name must be left‑justified and blank‑filled. It must consist of a dollar sign ($) followed by a process name in internal format. To indicate all processes, use a dollar sign and an asterisk ($*). program^file^name INT:value:12 is the name of the object file that the process to be measured is executing. The name must be left‑justified, blank‑filled, and in local ``` Struct Sqlstmt^desc (*); Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11] Int Run^unit[0:15];! procedure or run-unit name Int Stmt^index;! statement index number within the run-unit String Run^Unit^s = Run^unit; End; ``` internal format. You can use asterisks (*) in the volume, subvolume, and file-name fields within file^name. However, the volume name must begin with a dollar sign ($). For example, to specify all program file names, use the value "$* * *". run^unit INT:value:32 is the name of the procedure that contains the SQL statement to measure. To include all run units, the name of the procedure should be blank-filled. Do not use the asterisk (*) wild-card character. Not used for ANSI SQL. stmt^index INT:value:1 is the statement index number within the specified run unit (procedure). Not used for ANSI SQL. run^unit^s is the string representation of the run unit name. # SQLSTMT^OSS^DESCSQLSTMT^OSS^DESC is the descriptor for SQLSTMT entities that supports OSS file pathnames, SQL/MP procedures, or SQL/MX modules. ``` NOTENOTE When the measurement is being configured, only the process part of the descriptor is used; when performing a listing, all the fields can be used. ``` Definitions of the SQLSTMT^OSS^DESC fields that differ from the SQLSTMT^DESC descriptor follow. For definitions of all other fields, see SQLSTMT^DESC. PROGRAM^FILE^NAME^MID is a structure that contains the internal-format file name for the OSS pathname. pathid INT:value:12 is the internal format representation of an OSS file pathname for the specified program file name. To indicate all PATHIDs, initialize PATHID[0] to -1. crvsn ``` Struct Sqlstmt^OSS^desc (*); Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11]; Struct Program^file^name^MID;! Internal identifier for OSS pathname Begin Int Pathid[0:11];! Internal format OSS file pathname Int Crvsn[0:2];! Creation Volume Sequence Number End; Int Run^unit[0:63];! procedure or run-unit name Int Stmt^index;! statement index number within the run-unit String Run^Unit^s = Run^unit; End; ``` ``` INT:value:3 is the creation version serial number that identifies a unique instance of an OSS disk file. To indicate all CRVSN values, initialize CRVSN[0] to -1. ``` # SQLSTMT^ANSI^DESCSQLSTMT^ANSI^DESC is the descriptor for SQLSTMT entities that supports ANSI SQL file names. ``` NOTENOTE When an ANSI SQL name is copied into the descriptor’s run^unit field, it should not be enclosed in single quotes. ``` ##### NOTENOTE ``` When the measurement is being configured, only the process part of the descriptor is used; when performing a listing, all the fields can be used. ``` Definitions of the SQLSTMT^ANSI^DESC fields that differ from the SQLSTMT^DESC and SQLSTMT^OSS^DESC descriptors follow. For definitions of all the other SQLSTMT^ANSI^DESC fields, see SQLSTMT^DESC and SQLSTMT^OSS^DESC. version INT:value:1 is the sqlstmt^ANSI^desc^version to indicate this type of descriptor. stmt^index INT:value:1 is the statement index number within the ANSI SQL module. run^unit^name^len INT:value:1 is the actual length of the ANSI SQL module name in the run^unit field. ``` Struct Sqlstmt^ANSI^desc (*) Begin! Used for SQL/MX ANSI SQL name run-unit ! comparisons only. Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11]; Struct Program^file^name^MID;! Internal identifier for OSS pathname Begin Int Pathid[0:11];! Internal format OSS file pathname Int Crvsn[0:2];! Creation Volume Sequence Number End; Int Version;! Version of Sqlstmt desc; added in G11 ! Use the literal sqlstmt^ANSI^desc^version Int Stmt^index;! statement index number within the run-unit Int Run^unit^name^len; Int Minimum^ANSI^Run^unit[0:63];! desc is at least this long... Int Run^unit = Minimum^ANSI^Run^unit;! up to 384 words String Run^Unit^s = Run^unit; End; ``` ##### NOTENOTE ``` The ANSI SQL module name can be both shorter than and longer than 128 bytes. If it is longer than 128 bytes, you must make sure there is memory allocated for the "extra" space. ``` minimum^ANSI^run^unit[0:(min^wlen^ANSI^SQLNAME - 1)]; INT:value:64 where min^wlen^ANSI^SQLNAME is 64 words, which is the minimum length of this field. run^unit = minimum^ANSI^run^unit is the word representation of the run unit name. run^unit^s = run^unit is the string representation of the run unit name. NOTENOTE The run unit name can be any valid ANSI SQL module name, without enclosing single quotes. # SVNET^DESCSVNET^DESC is the descriptor for SERVERNET entities on systems running G-series and later RVUs. type INT:value:1 is the literal value SVNET^T or the numeric identifier 18. A positive type value includes the specified entities in the measurement configuration. A negative type value excludes the specified entities from measurement. For an example, see Specifying Entity Descriptors. len ``` Struct SvNet^desc (*) -- ServerNET descriptor structure BEGIN Int Type; -- controller entity type Int Len; -- byte length of the descriptor Int CPU^number; -- CPU number Int Ctrl^type; -- controller type Struct GMS; Begin Int(32) Group; -- Group number Int(32) Module; -- Module number Int(32) Remote^Cluster = Module; -- SuperCluster number Int(32) Slot; -- Slot number End; Int(32) Subdevice; -- controller type Int(32) Remote^CPU = Subdevice; -- IPC, RIPC destination CPU Struct PF = Subdevice; Begin Int Port; -- port number (for CLIM) Int Fiber; -- fiber number (for CLIM) End; Int(32) Node^class; -- controller type Int Config^name[0:31]; -- OSCONFIG name for adapter, SAC, or CLIM String Config^name^s[0:63] = Config^name; End; ``` INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor. len is 28 for an SVNET^DESC descriptor that does not include the configuration name or 92 for an SVNET^DESC descriptor that includes the configuration name. cpu^number INT:value:1 is the number of the CPU on which the SAC to be measured is configured. To indicate all CPUs, use the literal ‑1. ctrl^type INT:value:1 is the Hewlett Packard Enterprise product number for this SAC type. Use the literal ‑1 to indicate all SAC types. For information about the product number, see the documentation specific to each controller. Also 0 for a IPC/RIPC LINKER, 1 for a IPC/RIPC LISTENER, 2 to indicate CLMS activity to be recorded from Storage subsystem and 3 to indicate CLMS activity to be recorded from CIP subsystem. Literals for these values are in the files MEASDECS and MEASCHMA. GMS is the physical location address (group, module, slot) of the SAC to be measured. These are not used for measurements done on L- series RVUs. The GMS structure consists of these fields: group INT(32):value:1 is the group number. (The group corresponds to the physical enclosure.) To indicate all groups, use the literal ‑1. module INT(32):value:1 is the module number. To indicate all modules, use the literal ‑1. remote^cluster INT(32):value:1 redefines module is the ServerNet node number of the system with which the entity is communicating. To indicate all ServerNet cluster systems, use the literal -1. slot INT(32):value:1 is the slot number. To indicate all slots, use the literal ‑1. subdevice INT(32):value:1 is the subdevice number of the SAC to be measured. Use the literal ‑1 to indicate all SACs. For information about subdevice numbers, see the documentation specific to each SAC. remote^CPU INT(32):value:1 redefines subdevice is the number of the CPU on a remote ServerNet cluster with which the entity is communicating. To indicate all remote CPUs, use the literal -1. PF is the port-fiber structure for CLIM. As with the GMS field, this is not used for measurements on L-series RVUs. The PF structure consists of these fields: port INT:value:1 is the port number of the CLIM to be measured. To indicate all CLIMs, use the literal ‑1. fiber INT:value:1 is the fiber number of the of the CLIM to be measured. To indicate all CLIMs, use the literal ‑1. node^class INT(32):value:1 is the general class of SAC to be measured. On L-series RVUs, only node classes IPC, RIPC, CLIM, CLMI, CLMO, and CLMS are supported. The node‑class name must be enclosed in quotation marks and blank‑filled to four bytes: node^class^clim = "CLIM", node^class^clim^ip = "CLMI", node^class^clim^open = "CLMO", node^class^clim^storage = "CLMS", node^class^colo = "COLO", node^class^lan = "ENET", node^class^ipc = "IPC", node^class^mont = "MONT", node^class^nioc = "NIOC", node^class^ripc = "RIPC", node^class^scsi = "SCSI", node^class^wan = "SWAN", node^class^all = "* "; config^name INT:value:32 is the configuration name of the adapter, SAC, or CLIM to be measured, as defined by the system configuration database. config^name can also be the configuration name of an adapter that contains several ServerNet addressable controllers. In this case, the specification includes all devices of the requested type that are supported by that adapter. config^name must be null‑terminated and null‑filled. An asterisk in the first byte indicates all configuration names. To save space in the configuration table, you can omit config^name from the SVNET^DESC structure. You must set the descriptor length (len) to reflect whether or not you are using config^name because Measure uses the descriptor length to determine the start of the next descriptor in the configuration table. To not use config^name, set len to 28 and move only the first 28 bytes of the descriptor to the configuration table. To include config^name, set len to 92 and move the entire descriptor to the configuration table. config^name^s is the string representation of the configuration name. # SYSTEM^DESCSYSTEM^DESC is the descriptor for CLUSTER entities (on D-series RVUs only) or SYSTEM entities. type INT:value:1 ``` Struct System^desc (*) Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of this record Int LH^CPU;! Line handlers cpu number (primary or backup) Int System^number; Int System^name[0:3]; End; ``` ``` is one of: Entity TypeEntity TypeLiteralLiteral Numeric IdentifierNumeric Identifier SYSTEM REMSYS^T 11 CLUSTER CLUSTER^T 12 ``` A positive type value includes the specified entities in the measurement configuration. A negative type value excludes the specified entities from measurement. For an example, see Specifying Entity Descriptors. len INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor. lh^cpu INT:value:1 is the number of the CPU that contains the line handler (primary or backup). To indicate all CPUs, use the literal ‑1. system^number INT:value:1 is the system number of the remote system from which network traffic is to be measured. To indicate all systems, use the literal ‑1. system^name INT:value:4 is the system name of the remote system from which network traffic is to be measured. The name must be left‑justified and blank‑filled. It must consist of a backslash (\) followed by a system name in internal format. To indicate all systems, use a backslash followed by an asterisk (\*). # USERDEF^DESCUSERDEF^DESC is the descriptor for USERDEF entities. USERDEF^DESC is used with the COUNTER^DESC. For OSS file pathnames, use USERDEF^OSS^DESC. type INT:value:1 is the literal USERDEF^T or the numeric identifier 4. A positive type value includes the specified entities in the measurement configuration. A negative type value excludes the specified entities from measurement. For an example, see Specifying Entity Descriptors. len ``` Struct Userdef^desc (*); Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11]; Int Numcounters;! Number of counters to be installed End;! A subsequent counter^desc is not needed for an Entitydesc of ! a previously installed entity. But for Contab use, as many ! Counter^desc as desired must imediately follow this struct. ``` INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor and any COUNTER^DESC descriptors associated with it. See COUNTER^DESC. cpu^number INT:value:1 is the number of the CPU on which the process to be measured is running. pin INT:value:1 is the process identification number of the process to be measured. To indicate all PINs, use the literal ‑1. process^name INT:value:4 is the name of the process to measure. The name must be left‑justified and blank-filled. It must start with a dollar sign ($) and be in internal format. To indicate all processes, use a dollar sign followed by an asterisk ($*). program^file^name INT:value:12 is the name of the object file that the process to be measured is executing. The name must be left‑justified, blank‑filled, and in local internal format. To specify the volume, subvolume, and file-name fields within program^file^name, use asterisks (*). numcounters INT:value:1 is the number of COUNTER^DESC descriptors that follow. Each COUNTER^DESC descriptor describes a user‑defined counter to measure. NOTENOTE When passing a USERDEF^DESC descriptor to the MEASREAD or MEASREADACTIVE procedure, do not include COUNTER^DESC descriptors. # COUNTER^DESCCOUNTER^DESC describes a user‑defined counter specification. OSS file pathnames also use COUNTER^DESC. name INT:value:8 is a left‑justified, blank‑filled counter name. type INT:value:1 is one of these literal values: ``` Struct Counter^desc (*); ! Numcounters of these structs go here Begin! when used as a Contab descriptor Int Name[0:7];! User assigned counter name Int Type;! 1=accum, 2=busy, 3=queue Int Index;! 0:index counters will be allocated End; ``` ``` ACCUM Accumulating counter, 32 bits FACCUM Accumulating counter, 64 bits BUSY Busy counter QUEUE Queue counter QBUSY Queue Busy counter TCELLBUSY Busy counter, maintained with timer cells TCELLQUEUEQueue counter, maintained with timer cells TCELLQBUSYQueue Busy counter, maintained with timer cells SNAPSHOT Snapshot counter, 32 bits FSNAPSHOT Snapshot counter, 64 bits ``` index INT:value:1 is the highest index value required to address the array of counters associated with this counter name. If the counter is a single value, set index to 0. # USERDEF^OSS^DESCUSERDEF^OSS^DESC is the descriptor for USERDEF entities on systems running G06.12 and later RVUs. USERDEF^OSS^DESC includes support for OSS pathnames. USERDEF^OSS^DESC is also used with the COUNTER^DESC. Definitions of the USERDEF^OSS^DESC fields that differ from the USERDEF^DESC descriptor follow. For definitions of all the other USERDEF^OSS^DESC fields, see USERDEF^DESC. PROGRAM^FILE^NAME^MID is a structure that contains the internal-format file name for the OSS pathname. pathid INT:value:12 ``` Struct Userdef^OSS^desc (*); Begin Int Type;! Entity type Int Len;! Byte length of record. Int CPU^number; Int PIN; Int Process^name[0:3]; Int Program^file^name[0:11]; Struct Program^file^name^MID;! Internal identifier for OSS pathname Begin Int Pathid[0:11];! Internal format OSS file pathname Int Crvsn[0:2];! Creation Volume Sequence Number End; Int Numcounters;! Number of counters to be installed End;! A subsequent counter^desc is not needed for an Entitydesc of ! a previously installed entity. But for Contab use, as many ! Counter^desc as desired must immediately follow this struct. ``` ``` is the internal format representation of an OSS file pathname for the specified program file name. To indicate all PATHIDs, initialize PATHID[0] to -1. crvsn INT:value:3 is the creation version serial number that identifies a unique instance of an OSS disk file. To indicate all CRVSN values, initialize CRVSN[0] to -1. ``` # WAN^DESCWAN^DESC is the descriptor for LINE, NETLINE, or TERMINAL entities on systems running G‑series and later RVUs. type INT:value:1 is one of: Entity TypeEntity TypeLiteralLiteral Numeric IdentifierNumeric Identifier LINE LINE^T 9 NETLINE NETLINE^T 10 TERMINALTERM^T 13 A positive type value includes the specified entities in the measurement configuration. A negative type value excludes the specified entities from measurement. For an example, see Specifying Entity Descriptors. len INT:value:1 is the length in bytes of this descriptor. cpu^number INT:value:1 is the number of the CPU on which the measured line is configured. To indicate all CPUs, use the literal ‑1. trackid INT:value:3 is the 6‑byte ASCII identifier reported for the 3880 controller by the SCF subsystem (and usually printed on an external label as well). To indicate all TRACKIDs, use an asterisk (*) in the first byte and blank‑fill the rest of the field. clip ``` Struct WAN^desc (*) -- Line, Netline, and Terminal Descriptors Begin Int Type; -- Entity type Int Len; -- byte length of this structure Int CPU^number; Int Trackid[0:2]; -- Burned for SEEPROM String Trackid^s = trackid; Int Clip; -- 1 to 3 for SWAN, 1 to 6 for SWAN II Int Line; -- 0 or 1 Int Device^name[0:11]; String Device^name^s = device^name; Int Device^type; Int Device^subtype; End; ``` INT:value:1 is the number of the communications line interface processor (CLIP) being measured within the SWAN controller (1 through 3 for SWAN I, 1 through 6 for SWAN II). To indicate all CLIPs, use the literal ‑1. line INT:value:1 is the specific line controlled by a CLIP within a SWAN controller (0 or 1). To indicate both lines, use the literal ‑1. device^name INT:value:12 is the device name of the line being measured. The name must be left‑justified and blank‑filled. It must start with a dollar sign ($) and be in internal format. To indicate all devices, use a dollar sign followed by an asterisk ($*). device^name^s is the string representation of the device name. device^type INT:value:1 is the device type of a specific subsystem, such as 61 (X.25), as reported in the SCF LISTDEV listing. To indicate all devices, use the literal ‑1. device^subtype INT:value:1 is the device subtype of a specific subsystem, such as 62 (X25AM) as reported in the SCF LISTDEV listing. To indicate all devices, use the literal ‑1. # WebsitesWebsites ## General websitesGeneral websites Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center https://www.hpe.com/support/hpeschttps://www.hpe.com/support/hpesc Contact Hewlett Packard Enterprise Worldwide https://www.hpe.com/assistancehttps://www.hpe.com/assistance Subscription Service/Support Alerts https://www.hpe.com/support/e-updateshttps://www.hpe.com/support/e-updates For additional websites, see Support and other resources. # Support and other resourcesSupport and other resources SubtopicsSubtopics Accessing Hewlett Packard Enterprise SupportAccessing Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support HPE product registrationHPE product registration Accessing updatesAccessing updates Remote supportRemote support Warranty informationWarranty information Regulatory informationRegulatory information ``` Documentation feedbackDocumentation feedback ``` # Accessing Hewlett Packard Enterprise SupportAccessing Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support ``` For live assistance, go to the Contact Hewlett Packard Enterprise Worldwide website: https://www.hpe.com/info/assistancehttps://www.hpe.com/info/assistance To access documentation and support services, go to the Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center website: https://www.hpe.com/support/hpeschttps://www.hpe.com/support/hpesc ``` ## Information to collectInformation to collect ``` Technical support registration number (if applicable) Product name, model or version, and serial number Operating system name and version Firmware version Error messages Product-specific reports and logs Add-on products or components Third-party products or components ``` # HPEHPE product registration product registration To gain the full benefits of the Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center and your purchased support services, add your contracts and products to your account on the HPESC. ``` When you add your contracts and products, you receive enhanced personalization, workspace alerts, insights through the dashboards, and easier management of your environment. You will also receive recommendations and tailored product knowledge to self-solve any issues, as well as streamlined case creation for faster time to resolution when you must create a case. ``` To learn how to add your contracts and products, see https://www.hpe.com/info/add-products-contractshttps://www.hpe.com/info/add-products-contracts. # Accessing updatesAccessing updates ``` Some software products provide a mechanism for accessing software updates through the product interface. Review your product documentation to identify the recommended software update method. To download product updates: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center https://www.hpe.com/support/hpeschttps://www.hpe.com/support/hpesc My HPE Software Center https://www.hpe.com/software/hpesoftwarecenterhttps://www.hpe.com/software/hpesoftwarecenter ``` ``` To subscribe to eNewsletters and alerts: https://www.hpe.com/support/e-updateshttps://www.hpe.com/support/e-updates To view and update your entitlements, and to link your contracts and warranties with your profile, go to the Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center More Information on Access to Support Materials page: https://www.hpe.com/support/AccessToSupportMaterialshttps://www.hpe.com/support/AccessToSupportMaterials ``` ``` IMPORTANTIMPORTANT Access to some updates might require product entitlement when accessed through the Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center. You must have an HPE Account set up with relevant entitlements. ``` # Remote supportRemote support Remote support is available with supported devices as part of your warranty or contractual support agreement. It provides intelligent event diagnosis, and automatic, secure submission of hardware event notifications to Hewlett Packard Enterprise, which initiates a fast and accurate resolution based on the service level of your product. Hewlett Packard Enterprise strongly recommends that you register your device for remote support. If your product includes additional remote support details, use search to locate that information. HPE Get Connected https://www.hpe.com/services/getconnectedhttps://www.hpe.com/services/getconnected HPE Tech Care Service https://www.hpe.com/services/techcarehttps://www.hpe.com/services/techcare HPE Complete Care Service https://www.hpe.com/services/completecarehttps://www.hpe.com/services/completecare # Warranty informationWarranty information To view the warranty information for your product, see the warranty check toolwarranty check tool. # Regulatory informationRegulatory information To view the regulatory information for your product, view the Safety and Compliance Information for Server, Storage, Power, Networking, and Rack Products, available at the Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center: https://www.hpe.com/support/Safety-Compliance-EnterpriseProductshttps://www.hpe.com/support/Safety-Compliance-EnterpriseProducts ## Additional regulatory informationAdditional regulatory information Hewlett Packard Enterprise is committed to providing our customers with information about the chemical substances in our products as needed to comply with legal requirements such as REACH (Regulation EC No 1907/2006 of the European Parliament and the Council). A chemical information report for this product can be found at: https://www.hpe.com/info/reachhttps://www.hpe.com/info/reach For Hewlett Packard Enterprise product environmental and safety information and compliance data, including RoHS and REACH, see: https://www.hpe.com/info/ecodatahttps://www.hpe.com/info/ecodata For Hewlett Packard Enterprise environmental information, including company programs, product recycling, and energy efficiency, see: https://www.hpe.com/info/environmenthttps://www.hpe.com/info/environment # Documentation feedbackDocumentation feedback Hewlett Packard Enterprise is committed to providing documentation that meets your needs. To help us improve the documentation, use the Feedback button and icons (at the bottom of an opened document) on the Hewlett Packard Enterprise Support Center portal (https://www.hpe.com/support/hpeschttps://www.hpe.com/support/hpesc) to send any errors, suggestions, or comments. This process captures all document information. # Error MessagesError Messages The Measure subsystem produces error messages and error codes. The error messages generated by MEASCOM, the Measure command language interface, are described in this appendix. For descriptions of the error codes generated by Measure callable procedures, see Error Codes. Each message in this appendix is preceded by MEAS and the message number. If you encounter an error while using MEASCOM, enter: ## MEAS 2000MEAS 2000 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASCOM lets you get more information about a particular MEASURE message by entering HELP number. EffectEffect This message has no effect on the current processing. RecoveryRecovery None necessary; to suppress this comment, enter COMMENTS SUPPRESS 2000. ## MEAS 3000MEAS 3000 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The indicated system was not known to the network. EffectEffect The command is executed, but the corresponding system number or name could not be added to the descriptor. RecoveryRecovery Check that you correctly indicated the desired system. ## MEAS 3001MEAS 3001 ``` HELP error-number ``` ``` Comment. For more information type HELP number. ``` ``` WARNING. System system-name does not exist; check system name. ``` ``` WARNING. Device dev-name does not exist; check spelling. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The indicated device was not found or is not a valid device type for the command. EffectEffect The command is executed, but the device is not measured given the current state of the system. RecoveryRecovery Check that you correctly indicated the desired device or that the device is the correct type. ## MEAS 3002MEAS 3002 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The START MEASUREMENT command failed at some point during its processing. EffectEffect The reason for failure is listed as an error returned from the callable procedures. RecoveryRecovery Enter HELP error code number to determine the appropriate corrections, and retry the operation. (For information on ALLOCATESEGMENT, see the HPE Nonstop Guardian Procedure Calls Reference Manual.) ## MEAS 3003MEAS 3003 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The STOP MEASUREMENT command failed at some point during its processing. EffectEffect The reason for failure is listed as an error returned from the callable procedures. RecoveryRecovery Enter HELP error code number to determine the appropriate corrections, and retry the operation. (For information on ALLOCATESEGMENT, see the HPE Nonstop Guardian Procedure Calls Reference Manual.) ## MEAS 3004MEAS 3004 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause LOG STOP was issued, but logging was not in effect. EffectEffect The request is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Informative message only; no corrective action needed. ## MEAS 3005MEAS 3005 ``` WARNING. Measurement was not started due to reason listed above. ``` ``` WARNING. Measurement was not stopped due to reason listed above. ``` ``` WARNING. Logging was not on so command had no effect. ``` ``` WARNING. Changing current plot file to current data file. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ADD PLOT was issued, but the old data file associated with the plot was not the same as the one just listed from the current data file. EffectEffect The contents of the previous plot are lost. RecoveryRecovery Informative message only; no corrective action needed. ## MEAS 3006MEAS 3006 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A 7-character volume default was in effect with a remote system default. EffectEffect All file names expanded with the current defaults are invalid. RecoveryRecovery Change the system or volume default. ## MEAS 3007MEAS 3007 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An ADD PLOT was performed without any records available to be added. EffectEffect The request is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Perform a LIST entity command to obtain a list of the desired records before trying to add to the plot. ## MEAS 3008MEAS 3008 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to add more than 26 items to a plot. EffectEffect Some of the requested items were not added. RecoveryRecovery Delete any unneeded items so there are 26 or fewer before adding the desired counters. ## MEAS 3009MEAS 3009 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` WARNING. Volume not network-accessible with current system default. ``` ``` WARNING. No entities present to ADD; LIST records before trying ADD. ``` ``` WARNING. Plot became full during ADD; there can be a maximum of 26 entities; some entities were not ADDed. ``` ``` WARNING. Item not found -- item-name. ``` CauseCause The indicated plot item was not found or, if the plot character was also used in the command (for example, DELETE PLOT CPU-BUSY-TIME (A)), counter A was not an appropriate item name. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check that you spelled the item correctly and entered the correct plot character. ## MEAS 3010MEAS 3010 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The indicated data file was not found. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check that you spelled the file name correctly and that the indicated file was added. ## MEAS 3011MEAS 3011 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The indicated counter was not found. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check that you spelled the counter name correctly and that the indicated counter was added. ## MEAS 3012MEAS 3012 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The ADD MEASUREMENT command was issued for a data file that was previously added, and the MEASFH process is still active. EffectEffect The recent ADD MEASUREMENT command changes the current data file, but the MEASFH program file name remains unchanged. RecoveryRecovery To use a different MEASFH process on the same data file during a session, use the DELETE MEASUREMENT command to delete the current MEASFH process, then reissue the ADD MEASUREMENT command to specify a new MEASFH process. ## MEAS 3013MEAS 3013 ``` WARNING. Measurement not found -- filename. ``` ``` WARNING. Counter not found -- counter-name. ``` ``` WARNING. Measfh option ignored; DELETE then ADD to change Measfh. ``` WARNING. Measurement in the current data file is from _current‑datafile-version_ , which is a later release than this version ( _cu rrent-meascom-version_ ) of MEASCOM. ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Measurement in the current data file is from a later RVU than the version of the running MEASCOM. The later RVU might have changes in the external record format. EffectEffect The data file is added. RecoveryRecovery If you are certain there are no differences in the external record format between the two RVUs, you can disregard this warning message. Otherwise, use a MEASCOM from the same or later RVU of that in the data file. ## MEAS 3014MEAS 3014 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A three-digit sequence-ID was added to LOADID to designate each interval of the measurement when the LISTALL command is used. It was suppressed because three characters were not available for use. EffectEffect Sequencing is suppressed. RecoveryRecovery Use five or fewer characters for LOADID. ## MEAS 3015MEAS 3015 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASCOM is requesting an extended segment larger than 16 MB. EffectEffect The command continues unless a system error is encountered, in which case the error is displayed. RecoveryRecovery If the segment is considered to be too large, stop MEASCOM, and specify the LIST command in a way that decreases the number of records returned to MEASCOM. (For example, use the IF clause.) ## MEAS 3016MEAS 3016 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The item name entered is no longer maintained in the data file being processed. EffectEffect The command continues, but the operation might produce unexpected results. RecoveryRecovery Use an item name that is valid for the PVU of the data file being processed. ## MEAS 3017MEAS 3017 ``` WARNING. The specified Loadid has more than 5 characters, sequencing will be suppressed. ``` ``` WARNING. MEASCOM is attempting to allocate or resize its extended segment to be number bytes. ``` ``` WARNING. The item item-name is obsolete for the current datafile version; results of the operation may be unexpected. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The file name resolved in the LIST FILE command used the current default system, which is different from the system where the data file was collected. EffectEffect The command continues, but probably no data will be returned. RecoveryRecovery Enter the system name with the file name in the LIST command. For example, specify LIST FILE \A.$B.C.D, or specify SYSTEM \A and repeat the LIST command. ## MEAS 3018MEAS 3018 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause If displayed during a SHOW or SET command, the default attributes contain a value for both the TO and FOR options. If displayed during a LIST command, the default attributes plus the command line attributes caused the TO and FOR options to both be set. EffectEffect The command continues, but the FOR option is ignored. RecoveryRecovery To use the TO option, no recovery is necessary. Otherwise, use the RESET command to clear the TO value so the FOR value is used. ## MEAS 3019MEAS 3019 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASCTL was started with a SWAP volume other than $SYSTEM. If MEASCTL's SWAP volume is unavailable at any time (for example, if a PUP DOWN operation is performed), the entire system halts. EffectEffect The command is executed. RecoveryRecovery No recovery is mandatory. But if disk space permits, stop the MEASURE subsystem and restart it using $SYSTEM (or a mirrored volume) as the SWAP volume. Use this command: ## MEAS 3020MEAS 3020 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The command executed required a current measurement data file to perform its function. EffectEffect WARNING. The specified file name was resolved using the current default system ( _current-system-name_ ), which is different f rom the system where the data file was collected ( _system-name_ ). Therefore, this command may not LIST any records. ``` WARNING. TO and FOR are both set -- FOR will be ignored; if FOR is desired then RESET TO. ``` ``` WARNING. MEASCTL's current SWAP volume is volume ; do NOT bring volume down while MEASURE is still running. ``` ``` MEASCOM /SWAP $SYSTEM/ START MEASSUBSYS ``` ``` WARNING. No current data file; select a current data file using ADD. ``` The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Perform an ADD MEASUREMENT on the data file desired. ## MEAS 3021MEAS 3021 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause This command is valid only when the measurement is currently active. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Informative message only; no corrective action is needed. ## MEAS 3022MEAS 3022 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause There are no more entities configured for the type requested. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Restart the search or change to another entity type. ## MEAS 3023MEAS 3023 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A Guardian file name was specified in a LISTPNAME command without specifying the CRVSN qualifier. EffectEffect Translation of the Guardian file name to an OSS file pathname is attempted, but the reported name might be incorrect if the file name specified is a transient entity. RecoveryRecovery Include the CRVSN value as reported in Measure entity reports. ## MEAS 3024MEAS 3024 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An ADD MEASUREMENT command was entered for a data file that was still in the process of OSS journal segment construction at measurement shutdown. EffectEffect ``` WARNING. Measurement not active; unable to perform request. ``` ``` WARNING. No more configuration data is available. ``` ``` WARNING. CRVSN was not specified, translation may not be correct. ``` ``` WARNING. OSS journal segment is under construction, not attached. ``` The file is opened successfully, but the OSS journal segment is not attached. RecoveryRecovery Generally, no action is necessary. Translation can be processed from the OSS file-system name space on the current system. If you need access to the OSS journal segment at a later time, use the DELETE MEASUREMENT and ADD MEASUREMENT commands to delete and add the Measure data file again after OSS journal segment construction is complete. ## MEAS 3025MEAS 3025 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SQLSCHEMA command has been issued without specifying a default SQL catalog. The default SQL catalog has not yet been set. EffectEffect Although the default SQL schema is set, if no SQLCATALOG command is issued, the ANSI names used in the subsequent commands might fail with an error. RecoveryRecovery Issue the SQLCATALOG command to set the default SQL catalog. ## MEAS 3026MEAS 3026 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A START MEASUREMENT command was entered for a measurement that contains PROCESSH entities, and counter data record suppression was selected. EffectEffect The command continues, but PROCESSH records are not available for the measurement. RecoveryRecovery If PROCESSH records are desired, stop the measurement and restart it without counter data record suppression. If PROCESSH records are not desired, no corrective action is needed. ## MEAS 3027MEAS 3027 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A START MEASUREMENT command was entered for a measurement for which OSS and/or SQL journal segments were selected, and counter data record suppression was selected. EffectEffect The command continues, but OSS and/or SQL journal segments are not produced for the measurement. RecoveryRecovery If OSS and/or SQL journal segments are required, stop the measurement and restart it without counter data record suppression. If OSS and/or SQL journal segments are not required, no corrective action is needed. ## MEAS 3050MEAS 3050 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` WARNING. Default Catalog not set. ``` ``` WARNING. Counter data records have been suppressed; active counter data records are not available for PROCESSH. ``` ``` WARNING. Counter data records have been suppressed; OSS and/or SQL journal segments will not be produced. ``` ``` Exceeded depth of 4 OBEY files; rework OBEY nesting. ``` CauseCause An attempt was made to include more than four OBEY files. EffectEffect The command is aborted, and all OBEY files are closed. RecoveryRecovery Rework the nesting of your OBEY files so no more than four files are used. ## MEAS 3051MEAS 3051 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The string entered must contain, at most, the number of characters indicated by the error message. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Shorten the string appropriately and retry the command. ## MEAS 3052MEAS 3052 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A file name with a volume name containing more than seven characters was indicated for remote access. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery For remote access, a volume name can contain no more than seven characters. Shorten the volume name accordingly. ## MEAS 3053MEAS 3053 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The number entered was out of the acceptable range for the current item. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Enter a value within the range indicated by the error message. ## MEAS 3054MEAS 3054 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` Expecting a name of nn characters or less; shorten name. ``` ``` File is not network-accessible; shorten volume name. ``` ``` Value out of range; value must be 3000:3049, 3116, 3233, 3276, 3277 or 3299. ``` ``` System does not exist; check system name. ``` The system indicated in the request does not exist. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored because the file name cannot be expanded. RecoveryRecovery Check that you spelled the system name correctly. ## MEAS 3055MEAS 3055 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The parser encountered a syntax error. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Correct the syntax according to HELP or the syntax in this manual. For command syntax descriptions, see MEASCOM Commands. For descriptions of syntax for identifying entities to be measured, see Entities and Counters. ## MEAS 3056MEAS 3056 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A trap was encountered by MEASCOM. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Retain a copy of the information provided in the error message and contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEAS 3057MEAS 3057 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A DELETE PLOT was requested where the counter name entered occurs more than once in the plot definition. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Repeat the command and either add the plot character to indicate a particular occurrence or an asterisk (*) to indicate all occurrences. ## MEAS 3058MEAS 3058 ``` Illegal syntax; see HELP or the HPE Nonstop Measure Reference Manual. ``` Subsystem error; please report to Tandem Computers Incorporated. Trap % _n_ , CS = % _n_ , P = % _nnnnn_ , E = % _nnnnn_ , L = % _nn nnn_ , S = % _nnnnn_. ``` Ambiguous DELETE command; specify which occurrence to delete. ``` ``` Expecting a valid process or file name. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASCOM was expecting either a valid process name (for example, $XMM) or a valid file name (for example, $disk.subvol.file, or $disk.subvol.*). EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Reissue the request with a valid process or file name. ## MEAS 3059MEAS 3059 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A single file name was not entered for the command. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Replace the wild-card character (*) with a specific file name. ## MEAS 3060MEAS 3060 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A permanent disk file was not specified in the command. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Ensure the name entered is of the form $a.b.c. ## MEAS 3061MEAS 3061 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The process specification entered in the current ADD COUNTER command was not previously added by an ADD USERDEF command. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check that the process specification is correct, or add the USERDEF and retry the operation. ## MEAS 3062MEAS 3062 ``` Only an individual file can be specified; replace *'s. ``` ``` Expecting a disk file name; ensure filename is a valid disk file name. ``` ``` The USERDEF for this counter was not found; ADD the USERDEF. ``` ``` Counter already exists; DELETE and then ADD to change counter. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to add a counter that already exists. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery To change the counter type or array index, delete the counter and then add it. ## MEAS 3063MEAS 3063 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to add more than 64 data files. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Delete any unnecessary data files and then add the ones desired. ## MEAS 3064MEAS 3064 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A valid or unique disk file was not specified in the command. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Ensure that the name entered is of the form $a.b.c or $a.#x. ## MEAS 3065MEAS 3065 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The file name given or expanded was remote, and the command required a local file name. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery For a discussion of file names that are used with the command, see the description of the specific command in MEASCOM Commands. Then specify a local file and retry the operation. ## MEAS 3066MEAS 3066 ``` No more data files can be added; DELETE unnecessary files. ``` ``` Expecting a disk file name; ensure file is a valid disk filename. ``` ``` File must be local; see HPE Nonstop Measure Reference Manual. ``` ``` The name specified in the IF condition ( item-name ) was not found; check the spelling of the name. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The item name used in the IF condition was not an item name associated with the entity type being listed. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check the spelling of the item and verify the item name exists, then retry the operation. ## MEAS 3067MEAS 3067 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The item name used in the BY condition was not an item name associated with the entity type being listed. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check the spelling of the item and verify the item name exists, then retry the operation. ## MEAS 3068MEAS 3068 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The command executed required a current measurement data file to perform its function. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Perform an ADD MEASUREMENT on the data file desired. ## MEAS 3069MEAS 3069 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An option was specified more than once in the same command. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Delete all but one occurrence of the option and repeat the command. ## MEAS 3070MEAS 3070 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` The name specified in the BY clause ( item-name ) was not found; check the spelling of the name. ``` ``` No current data file; select a current data file using ADD. ``` ``` Command option has been repeated; delete one and reissue command. ``` ``` Both FOR and TO have been specified; delete one and reissue command. ``` CauseCause Both the FOR and TO option were specified in the same command. These options are mutually exclusive. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Delete one of the options and repeat the command. ## MEAS 3071MEAS 3071 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The plot counter name entered was not found. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check the spelling and verify, using the appropriate report, that the name entered is a valid item name. Legacy style report items that are no longer present in the equivalent ZMS style report can only be plotted in the legacy style interface. And ZMS style report items that are not present in the equivalent legacy style report can only be plotted in the ZMS style interface. ## MEAS 3072MEAS 3072 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to issue a command that is valid only when the measurement is currently active. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Informative message only; no corrective action is needed. ## MEAS 3073MEAS 3073 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The file name entered or expanded (using the current SYSTEM) for a DISCOPEN or DISKFILE command was for a remote file. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery DISCOPENs and DISKFILEs are always local. For DISCOPENs, a remote opener can be specified after the file name (for example, $a.b.c (\sys)). For DISKFILE, no opener information is associated with the entity, so using a remote opener is not allowed. ## MEAS 3074MEAS 3074 ``` item-name not found; check the spelling and/or the appropriate entity report. ``` ``` Measurement not active; unable to perform request. ``` ``` A remote filename is not allowed when dealing with DISCOPENs or DISKFILEs. ``` ``` Plot list is full; must DELETE an item before ADD. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to add an item to an already full plot. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Delete some counters and then retry the ADD. ## MEAS 3075MEAS 3075 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The plot character was not a single alphabetic character. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Enter only a single alphabetic character. For more information, enter HELP. ## MEAS 3079MEAS 3079 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The measurement space became full during an ADD. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Delete unneeded descriptors from the measurement space and retry the operation. ## MEAS 3080MEAS 3080 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An error was encountered and returned from segment_allocate64_ or segment_resize64_ for the MEASCOM extended work segment. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery MEASCOM can run without the segment, but it can perform only in a limited capacity. Change the command which is causing the error. Use the general time limitation parameters such as FROM, TO, FOR and so on to limit the number of records returned. For most entities, specify a specific CPU, DEVICE, DISK, DISKFILE and so on. If the error persists, check the error code and detailed error code displayed, and take the appropriate action. If the error code and detailed error code are not helpful in resolving the error, contact Global Nonstop Support Center (GNSC). ``` Expecting a single alpha character; see HELP. ``` ``` Measurement space overflow; DELETE unneeded descriptors. ``` ``` Allocate segment error on extended work segment. ``` ## MEAS 3081MEAS 3081 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Use of the IF clause on a non-counter item is not supported for data files prior to D10. EffectEffect The requested operation is aborted. RecoveryRecovery Use an item name that corresponds to a counter item, or delete the IF clause. ## MEAS 3083MEAS 3083 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause When Measure expanded the command, a buffer became full. EffectEffect The requested command is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Break the command into several commands and execute the commands separately, or qualify any file names present so MEASCOM performs a minimum of expansion. ## MEAS 3084MEAS 3084 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The current file name is not a valid disk file name EffectEffect The requested command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the file name entered to a valid permanent or temporary disk file name. ## MEAS 3085MEAS 3085 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The symbol displayed by the error message is not a valid word separator for the string entered. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Either remove the character or replace it with a valid word separator, and then retry the command. ``` The IF clause on an identifier field (i.e. name ) is not supported for the current datafile version. ``` ``` Internal buffer full; try making command less complex. ``` The DISCOPEN and DISKFILE entities require the filename to be either a permanent or temporary disk filename (e.g. $a.b.* o r $system.#*). ``` " char " not allowed in current string; delete symbol and retry. ``` ## MEAS 3086MEAS 3086 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause START was attempted on a data file that was added for listing. Because the START command causes the data to be purged, the command is aborted. The commands START, STOP, or ADD cause the data file to be added in the MEASCOM environment. This message is an attempt to prevent accidental purging of data. EffectEffect The requested operation is aborted. RecoveryRecovery If you still need the START operation, DELETE the data file and retry the START. ## MEAS 3087MEAS 3087 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The key string entered for the HELP command exceeds 30 characters. EffectEffect The requested operation is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Shorten the string. ## MEAS 3089MEAS 3089 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The number entered has more than three decimal digits. EffectEffect The requested operation is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Reduce the number of decimal digits, then reenter the command. ## MEAS 3090MEAS 3090 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The PROCESSH record format in the data file is incompatible with the MEASCOM currently running. EffectEffect The requested operation is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Obtain a proper version of MEASCOM. ``` START attempted on an ADDed datafile; DELETE and then START. ``` ``` Expecting Help key length of 30 characters or less; shorten string. ``` ``` Expecting a number with 3 or less decimal digits. ``` ``` Incompatible PROCESSH records in the filename data file. Please use a proper version of MEASCOM. ``` ## MEAS 3091MEAS 3091 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The record size is different from the size in the data file. EffectEffect The requested operation is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Remove the existing structured file and let MEASCOM create it, or use a different subvolume. Be sure to regenerate the corresponding DDL dictionary. ## MEAS 3092MEAS 3092 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause SYSTEM-PROCESSES is not allowed in SQLPROC and SQLSTMT commands. EffectEffect The requested operation is not performed. RecoveryRecovery Change the command to a suitable format and retry. ## MEAS 3094MEAS 3094 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause LISTALL command was issued on a C10 or previous data file. EffectEffect Command aborts; specified MEASFH cannot satisfy the request. RecoveryRecovery Try another command. To retrieve records, use the LIST command. ## MEAS 3095MEAS 3095 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Illegal list-option was specified for the LISTALL command. EffectEffect Command aborts; specified option is not satisfied. RecoveryRecovery Retry the command, and do not include the list-option causing the error. ``` The existing DDL format structured file for the entity specified in the LIST command has a different record size. ``` ``` SYSTEM-PROCESSES is not allowed in this command. ``` ``` Incompatible datafile version. The LISTALL command is not supported for this version of the datafile. ``` ``` Command option " list-option " not allowed with LISTALL command when STRUCTURED format is specified. ``` ## MEAS 3096MEAS 3096 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The TOLERANCE OFF option was specified on a C10 or previous data file. EffectEffect Command aborts; MEASFH cannot satisfy request. RecoveryRecovery Retry the command, and do not include the TOLERANCE option. ## MEAS 3097MEAS 3097 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause When data file was added, MEASFH reported an error or warning. Problems might exist with the data file. EffectEffect Command aborts; command could not be carried out correctly. RecoveryRecovery Try a different command on this data file. ## MEAS 3098MEAS 3098 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Although an effective record can contain up to 2100 characters, only effective records of 132 characters or fewer are candidates for modification using the FC command or for reexecution using the! command. EffectEffect No command is executed. RecoveryRecovery The desired command must be entered in full (use the editor and the OBEY command to execute long commands). ## MEAS 3099MEAS 3099 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The line that Measure is attempting to be reexecute is not in the HISTORY buffer. EffectEffect No command is executed. RecoveryRecovery Perform a HISTORY command to make sure the command is still in the HISTORY buffer. Otherwise you must reenter the command in full. ``` Command option "TOLERANCE OFF" not allowed with LIST command. ``` ``` Error number was reported earlier on this data file. Operation cannot be carried out. ``` ``` FC and! are not allowed on input longer than 132 characters; reenter the desired command in full. ``` ``` Line specified is not in the HISTORY buffer; check buffer and retry. ``` ## MEAS 3100MEAS 3100 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Even with the use of the ampersand (&), there is a limit to the number of characters that can be entered in one record before MEASCOM scans the record and executes the commands. The current input stream exceeded the limit of 2100 characters. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery If more than one command was entered, try entering each command separately. If only one command was entered, try breaking the command into several commands and execute the commands separately. If the command cannot be decomposed, try removing some embedded spaces to make the command shorter. ## MEAS 3101MEAS 3101 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The program file indicated was not started due to the Process_Create_ error indicated. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Correct the cause of the Process_Create_ error and retry the command. ## MEAS 3102MEAS 3102 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The program file indicated could not be opened due to the file-system error indicated. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Correct the cause of the file-system error and retry the command. ## MEAS 3103MEAS 3103 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The program file indicated did not read the startup message sent due to the file-system error indicated. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery The command is too long, the maximum number of characters an effective record can contain is 2100 ( _nnnn_ were entered); try to enter multiple commands or remove embedded spaces as possible to shorten the command(s) entered. ``` filename was not started due to Process_Create_ error number1 ( number2 ); correct error and retry command. ``` ``` filename could not be opened due to File System error number. ``` ``` filename did not read the startup message due to File System error number. ``` Correct the cause of the file-system error and retry the command. ## MEAS 3104MEAS 3104 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The effective value for the SCALE-FROM attribute was greater than or equal to the SCALE-TO attribute of the PLOT object. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Perform a SHOW PLOT to determine the default values for each attribute. Any attribute entered on the LIST PLOT command line overrides the default value. Ensure the effective values are consistent when you retry the command. ## MEAS 3105MEAS 3105 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The word encountered is not a valid keyword. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Enter a valid keyword (some are displayed in the error text, but not all if the list is too long). ## MEAS 3106MEAS 3106 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An item name was used in the IF clause that can only be used in the BY clause. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Use a different item name, or use a counter name that appears within the entity report. ## MEAS 3107MEAS 3107 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An item name was used in a PLOT command that can only be used in the BY and IF clauses. EffectEffect The command is not executed. ``` SCALE-FROM must be less than SCALE-TO; make sure the values are correct before retrying the LIST PLOT command. ``` ``` Expecting one of the following keywords keyword-list. ``` _item-name_ is a valid item name, but it can only be used in the BY clause; use a different item name, or a counter name that appears within the entity report. _item-name_ is a valid item name, but it can only be used in the By and IF clauses; use a counter name that appears within th e entity report. RecoveryRecovery Use a counter name that appears within the entity report. ## MEAS 3108MEAS 3108 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The item name entered matched more than one item name. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Add more characters to the name entered. ## MEAS 3109MEAS 3109 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An item name was entered that is not contained in the (down-rev) data file being processed. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Use a valid item name. ## MEAS 3110MEAS 3110 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The digit entered is too large for the current base (for example, ADD CPU %8 is incorrect because an octal digit must be 7 or under). EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the number entered to be consistent with the base you are using. ## MEAS 3111MEAS 3111 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The code map specified is incorrect for native mode code maps. EffectEffect _item-name_ is not a unique item-name; do a HELP _entity-type_ COUNTERS (e.g. HELP CPU COUNTERS) to determine the numb er of characters required to make the abbreviation unique. ``` item-name is a new item name that is not valid for the current datafile version; use a different item name. ``` The current string of digits contains a digit which is greater than the current base of _number_ ; delete the digit and retry the c ommand. Native Mode code maps do not have space numbers; if you wish to sample native mode maps specify either UCr, ULr, SCr, o r SLr. The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery To measure native mode code, specify UCr, ULr, SCr, or SLr. ## MEAS 3112MEAS 3112 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause You are using a G-series RVU, but the version of the data file precedes F40.00. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery To analyze a pre-F40.00 data file, use a pre-F40.00 version of MEASCOM that matches the version of the data file as well as the appropriate MEASFH. ## MEAS 3113MEAS 3113 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFH was unable to open the data file because there was too much data for the system to handle (file-system error 21). EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Retry the command. ## MEAS 3114MEAS 3114 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A command was entered for a data file that was not collected on the current node, and for which no journal segment is currently available. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery If the translation desired is NOT from the added data file, then DELETE the measurement and reissue the command so that the translation is done from the current system. If translation is desired for data collected on another node, specify the OSS and/or SQL option when starting the measurement. ## MEAS 3115MEAS 3115 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An invalid PAGESIZE setting was specified. ``` The datafile version is version and cannot be analyzed with F40 Meascom. ``` ``` Unable to open data file. ``` ``` A journal segment is required but not available for request. ``` ``` PAGESIZE must be specified in the range of 6 and 128 inclusive. ``` EffectEffect The requested operation is aborted. RecoveryRecovery Specify a value from 6 through 128 inclusive. ## MEAS 3116MEAS 3116 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The requested translation was not found in the OSS name space or in the current OSS journal segment. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery If the request is part of an ADD data file that contains an OSS journal segment, you can DELETE the data file and attempt translation from the current system if the system is the same as that of the ADD data file. ## MEAS 3117MEAS 3117 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The requested translation was not found in the ANSI SQL name space or in the current ANSI SQL journal segment. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery If the request is part of an ADD data file that contains an ANSI SQL journal segment, you can delete the data file and attempt translation from the current system if the system is the same as that of the data file. For the command LISTACTIVE DISKFILE, make sure that a partition is specified. Otherwise, no recovery is possible. ## MEAS 3118MEAS 3118 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The ANSI SQL name contained syntax errors. EffectEffect The requested operation is aborted. RecoveryRecovery Reenter command with correctly formed ANSI SQL name. ## MEAS 3121MEAS 3121 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` Unable to translate OSS file pathname. ``` ``` Unable to translate an ANSI SQL name. ``` ``` Syntax errors in specified ANSI SQL name. ``` ``` Default Catalog or Schema missing. ``` There is not enough information available to create a fully qualified SQL ANSI name. Either the default SQL catalog, the default SQL schema, or both are missing. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Issue the ENV command to see what the settings are for default SQL catalog and SQL schema. Issue the appropriate SQLCATALOG and/or SQLSCHEMA command(s) to add the missing default setting(s). ## MEAS 3122MEAS 3122 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A LIST command was issued for the DISC or DEVICE entity that specified a CLIM device or path selection on a pre-H03 data file. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the LIST command to not specify a CLIM device, path selection, lun, or partition number. ## MEAS 3123MEAS 3123 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The MEASCOM extended work segment has reached maximum capacity and cannot be extended. EffectEffect The requested operation is ignored. RecoveryRecovery Change the command which is causing the error. Use the general time limitation parameters such as FROM, TO, FOR and so on to limit the number of records returned. For most entities, specify a specific CPU, DEVICE, DISK, DISKFILE and so on. If the error persists, contact Global Nonstop Support Center (GNSC). ## MEAS 3124MEAS 3124 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The ADD DEVICE, LIST DEVICE, LISTALL DEVICE, and LISTACTIVE DEVICE commands do not allow the partition number to be specified. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the command to not specify a partition number. ## MEAS 3125MEAS 3125 ``` The data file format does not support CLIM devices, or path selection. ``` ``` Maximum available memory for work segment exceeded. ``` ``` Partition number is not allowed for this command and entity. ``` ``` A sac-list specification is not allowed for this command and entity. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The ADD DEVICE, ADD DISC, LIST DEVICE, LISTALL DEVICE, LISTACTIVE DEVICE, and LISTACTIVE DISC commands do not allow multiple CLIM names to be specified. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the command to not specify multiple CLIM names. ## MEAS 3126MEAS 3126 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The ADD DEVICE, LIST DEVICE, LISTALL DEVICE, and LISTACTIVE DEVICE commands do not allow the path suffix to be specified. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the command to not specify a path suffix. ## MEAS 3127MEAS 3127 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The LIST command STYLE LEGACY is not supported for this entity. EffectEffect The command is not executed. RecoveryRecovery Change the command to not specify STYLE LEGACY. ## MEAS 3128MEAS 3128 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause STYLE LEGACY is not allowed with the current report format. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Either specify STYLE ZMS or change the report format. ## MEAS 3129MEAS 3129 ``` Path is not allowed for this command and entity. ``` ``` (ERR^LEGACY^NOT^SUPPORTED) ``` ``` STYLE ZMS is required for the current report format ``` ``` File System error nnnn occurred while opening or accessing the data export file. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A File System error occurred while opening or accessing the data export file. EffectEffect The data export file is either not created or is incomplete. RecoveryRecovery If the output file already exists, ensure that it can be overwritten. If it doesn’t exist, ensure that there is adequate space on the disk volume and that writes are permitted to it. ## MEAS 3130MEAS 3130 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The data export file already exists and has an incorrect type or file code. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Delete, move, or rename the file. ## MEAS 3175MEAS 3175 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to run MEASCOM on a system other than a Nonstop server. EffectEffect MEASCOM stops abruptly. RecoveryRecovery See your system manager. ## MEAS 3176MEAS 3176 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An attempt was made to run MEASCOM with an option number other than T2035. EffectEffect MEASCOM stops abruptly. RecoveryRecovery See your system manager. ## MEAS 3177MEAS 3177 ``` An existing file that MEASCOM tries to open for data export has incorrect type or file code. ``` ``` System is not TANDEM NonStop; see your system manager. ``` ``` System option number is not T2035; see your system manager. ``` ``` Guardian version is not B30 or greater; see your system manager. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Nonstop operating system PVU must be B30 or later. EffectEffect MEASCOM stops abruptly. RecoveryRecovery See your system manager. ## MEAS 3178MEAS 3178 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The internal check nnn failed. EffectEffect MEASCOM stops abruptly. RecoveryRecovery If the command involves a great deal of file-name expansion, rerun MEASCOM and try to perform the same command in several steps. If this attempt fails or does not address the original problem, contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. # Error CodesError Codes The Measure subsystem produces error messages and error codes. The error codes generated by Measure callable procedures are described in this appendix. For descriptions of the error messages generated by MEASCOM, the Measure command interface, see Error Messages. ## 32003200 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The MEASCB was not set to -1s on the initial procedure call or its contents were modified after being set by Measure. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Initialize MEASCB to all -1s prior to your first Measure call and do not write to it afterwards. ## 32013201 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A required parameter was missing. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. ``` Internal consistency check nnn failed; report error. ``` ``` (ERR^BADMEASCB) ``` ``` (ERR^MISSINGPARAM) ``` RecoveryRecovery Add the missing parameter to your procedure call statement. ## 32023202 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An invalid parameter address was supplied. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Check and correct the address initialization of your parameters. ## 32033203 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A bad parameter value was supplied to a callable procedure. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Check and correct the values of your parameters. ## 32043204 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The buffer size was too small. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Correct the buffer size. The buffer size needed is contained in the bytesret (byte size of record returned) parameter. If the MEASREADACTIVE procedure encountered this error and requires a buffer larger than 32 KB, use the MEAS_READACTIVE_ procedure. ## 32053205 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Not enough data-stack space was available to send or receive the buffer. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. ``` (ERR^BOUNDS) ``` ``` (ERR^BADPARAMS) ``` ``` (ERR^BUFTOOSMALL) ``` ``` (ERR^NOTENOUGHSTACK) ``` RecoveryRecovery Move some of the stack data into an extended segment to increase the size of the available working stack. ## 32063206 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An unexpected reply was received from a Measure process. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Retain a copy of the files and commands needed to duplicate this error and contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## 32073207 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A down-rev MEASFH being used cannot support the new field used in the descriptor. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Reissue the request without using the new field. In MEASCOM, avoid the new fields. For example, change LIST FILE * ($PNAME) to LIST FILE * (1,52), assuming 1,52 was $PNAME. If you are using the programmatic interface directly, use the old version of the descriptor, or place only the appropriate default values (for example, $*) in the new fields. ## 32083208 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The MEASFH process, which indexes and builds structured records from the data file, and the data file are on different systems. EffectEffect The requested operation aborts. RecoveryRecovery Move either the MEASFH program file or the data file so they are both on the same node. Only read access is allowed for remote data files. ## 32093209 ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The data file was not on the same system as the calling process.
