In this case it puts PROCESS and FILE records in one file and all other records in another file. ``` ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 63> ``` ``` MEASURE File Tool - T9086J04 - (01AUG12) - \BLPROD (C) Copyright 2007-2012 Hewlett-Packard Development Company, L.P. SPLIT MDFALLI,MIN15,BY TIME,FROM 11:15,FOR 15 MINUTES File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFALLI Time period: 16 Mar 2012, 10:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 14:32:34 File Size % Time Period ---------------------------------- ---------- ------ -------------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.MIN15 96185940 10.68 from 16 Mar 2012, 11:15:00 to 16 Mar 2012, 11:30:00 ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDFALLI,MIN15,BY TIME,FROM 11:15,FOR 15 MINUTES ``` ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 149> ``` ``` MEASURE File Tool - T9086J04 - (01AUG12) - \BLPROD (C) Copyright 2007-2012 Hewlett-Packard Development Company, L.P. SPLIT MDFALLI,MDFA*,BY TIME,INTO 1 HOUR PARTS File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFALLI Time period: 16 Mar 2012, 10:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 14:32:34 File Size % Time Period ---------------------------------- ---------- ------ -------------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFA00 255728012 28.40 from 16 Mar 2012, 10:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 11:26:09 $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFA01 278494900 30.93 from 16 Mar 2012, 11:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 12:26:09 $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFA02 190677148 21.18 from 16 Mar 2012, 12:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 13:26:09 $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFA03 182919124 20.31 from 16 Mar 2012, 13:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 14:26:09 $PERF.CAPAPP.MDFA04 25952028 2.88 from 16 Mar 2012, 14:26:09 to 16 Mar 2012, 14:32:34 ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDFALLI,MDFA*,BY TIME,INTO 1 HOUR PARTS ``` ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 7> MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT MDATA5 File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDATA5 was split into the following files: ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDATA5 ``` Split Measure data file MDATA5 by entity, with each entity going into a separate output data file. Let MEASFT name the output data files. Split Measure data file MDATA5 by entity, with FILE data going into a file named SFFILE and the rest going into SFREST. Split Measure data file MDATA5 by entity, with CPU and PROCESS data going into a file named SFFILE1 and SERVERNET data going into SFFILE2. ``` File Size % Entity(s) ----------------------------------- ---------- ------ --------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF000 55990206 41.79 CPU,DISCOPEN,DISC, NETLINE,TMF,SQLPROC, SQLSTMT,SERVERNET, DISKFILE,OSSCPU,OSSNS $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF001 76588122 57.16 PROCESS,FILE ``` ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 8> ``` ``` MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT MDATA5,*,BY ENTITY * File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDATA5 was split into the following files: File Size % Entity(s) ----------------------------------- ---------- ------ --------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF000 133842 0.10 CPU $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF001 41239732 30.78 PROCESS $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF004 35349176 26.38 FILE $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF005 25679600 19.17 DISCOPEN $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF006 2032272 1.52 DISC $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF009 174430 0.13 NETLINE $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF013 30552 0.02 TMF $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF014 10742 0.01 SQLPROC $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF015 83114 0.06 SQLSTMT $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF017 10586256 7.90 SERVERNET $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF018 17086666 12.75 DISKFILE $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF019 156412 0.12 OSSCPU $PERF.CAPAPP.ZMSRF020 24180 0.02 OSSNS ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDATA5,*,BY ENTITY * ``` ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 10> ) MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT MDATA5,(SFFILE,SFREST),BY ENTITY (FILE,*) File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDATA5 was split into the following files: File Size % Entity(s) ----------------------------------- ---------- ------ --------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.SFFILE 35349176 26.38 FILE $PERF.CAPAPP.SFREST 97229152 72.57 CPU,PROCESS,DISCOPEN, DISC,NETLINE,TMF, SQLPROC,SQLSTMT, SERVERNET,DISKFILE, OSSCPU,OSSNS ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDATA5,(SFFILE,SFREST),BY ENTITY (FILE,* ``` ``` Split Measure data file MDATA5 by processor number, with each processor's data going into a separate output data file. The name of the output data files start with "MCPU". ``` # HELP CommandHELP Command The HELP command displays help information for the published commands. SubtopicsSubtopics SyntaxSyntax # SyntaxSyntax Enter "MEASFT HELP" or just "MEASFT" to display help text for the MEASFT command. # MEASFT Error and Warning MessagesMEASFT Error and Warning Messages Any of the following messages can appear on the console when you run MEASFT. This example illustrates the format of a typical error and warning: ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 14> ``` ``` MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT MDATA5,(SFFILE1,SFFILE2),BY ENTITY (CPU PROCESS,SERVERNET) File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDATA5 was split into the following files: File Size % Entity(s) ----------------------------------- ---------- ------ --------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.SFFILE1 41372788 30.88 CPU,PROCESS $PERF.CAPAPP.SFFILE2 10586256 7.90 SERVERNET ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDATA5,(SFFILE1,SFFILE2), BY ENTITY (CPU PROCESS,SERVERNET) ``` ``` $PERF.CAPAPP 16> ``` ``` MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT MDATA5,MCPU*,BY CPU * File $PERF.CAPAPP.MDATA5 was split into the following files: File Size % CPU(s) ----------------------------------- ---------- ------ --------------------- $PERF.CAPAPP.MCPU00 28410588 21.21 0 $PERF.CAPAPP.MCPU01 19602928 14.63 1 $PERF.CAPAPP.MCPU02 41879174 31.26 2 $PERF.CAPAPP.MCPU03 42687210 31.86 3 ``` ``` MEASFT SPLIT MDATA5,MCPU*,BY CPU * ``` ``` HELP | empty ``` If a message indicates a syntax error, a circumflex (^) shows where the syntax error was detected. ## MEASFT 2000 (WARN_SPLIT_FILE_NOT_CREATED)MEASFT 2000 (WARN_SPLIT_FILE_NOT_CREATED) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The specified output data file was not created because MEASFT found no data records to put into it. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues, but the specified output file is not created. RecoveryRecovery Remove the specified output file and its corresponding split criteria from the command line. ## MEASFT 2001 (WARN_SPLIT_FILE_PURGED)MEASFT 2001 (WARN_SPLIT_FILE_PURGED) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The output file name specified (either directly or indirectly through a wildcard) already exists and is not the same as the input data file, the file that is to be split. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues, and previous data is overwritten. RecoveryRecovery It is too late to recover the data in the overwritten file. In the future, specify an output file name that doesn't conflict with existing files. ## MEASFT 2002 (WARN_NO_XRTIMES_AT_EOF)MEASFT 2002 (WARN_NO_XRTIMES_AT_EOF) ``` MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT KMEAS3, ,BY CPU * ^ *** Error 3232: SPLIT name Resolution failed, File System Error = #13 $PERF KMZMFT 45> MEASFT SPLIT KMEAS2,*,BY ENTITY (CPU TERMINAL,*) MEASURE File Tool - T9086H01 - (01AUG06) - \YOSPRD Copyright 2006 Hewlett-Packard Company SPLIT KMEAS2,*,BY ENTITY (CPU TERMINAL,*) *** Warning 2007: No data for this specified entity, TERMINAL File $PERF.KMZMFT.KMEAS2 was split into the following files: File Size % Entity(s) ------------------------------- ---------- ------ ---------------------- $PERF.KMZMFT.ZMSRF000 118098 0.60 CPU $PERF.KMZMFT.ZMSRF001 18679336 95.33 PROCESS,PROCESSH, SERVERNET ``` ``` Specified file, out-file , not created because of lack of data records. ``` ``` Specified output filename, out-file , already existed, previous data was overwritten. ``` ``` No Stop Measurement record, file possibly truncated/corrupted. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause There is no Stop Measurement record in the input data file. The file is either a truncated copy or the file was corrupted. EffectEffect The INFO or SPLIT command continues. RecoveryRecovery Determine the cause of the missing record and decide whether this is a significant issue. ## MEASFT 2003 (WARN_BAD_SVNET_ENT_LEN)MEASFT 2003 (WARN_BAD_SVNET_ENT_LEN) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A record for the ENDPOINT/SERVERNET, DISC, or DEVICE entity, entity-name, had an unexpected length, length. The record number is number. EffectEffect The INFO or SPLIT command continues. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative about this warning and save the data file that caused it. ## MEASFT 2004 (WARN_BAD_ENT_LEN)MEASFT 2004 (WARN_BAD_ENT_LEN) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A record for the entity, entity-name, had an unexpected length, length. The record number is number. EffectEffect The INFO or SPLIT command continues. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative about this warning and save the data file that caused it ## MEASFT 2005 (WARN_TOO_MANY_SPLIT_FILES)MEASFT 2005 (WARN_TOO_MANY_SPLIT_FILES) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Measure data file contains data records for fewer entities than split files specified so MEASFT cannot do the split as specified. This warning occurs only when the user specifies a definite number of output files but no split criteria, forcing MEASFT to determine the split criteria. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. MEASFT ignores the extra output file names. RecoveryRecovery Decrease the number of output data files specified. ## MEASFT 2006 (WARN_NO_SPLIT_FILES_CREATED)MEASFT 2006 (WARN_NO_SPLIT_FILES_CREATED) ``` Bad length in ENDPT/SVNET, DISC or DEVICE entity, entity-name , length # length , record # number ``` ``` Bad length in entity, entity-name , length # length , record # number ``` ``` More output files specified than entities present; # files lowered from mm to nn ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause None of the records in the Measure data file satisfied any of the split criteria so no output files were created. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. RecoveryRecovery Determine the cause of the missing records and decide whether this is a significant issue. ## MEASFT 2007 (WARN_ENTITY_HAS_NO_DATA)MEASFT 2007 (WARN_ENTITY_HAS_NO_DATA) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause No data records were encountered for an entity that was specified in the split criteria. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. If the data file contained no data for any entity to be represented in a given output data file--for example, if an output file is to contain data for PROCESS and DISK entities, but the data file being split contains no records for either of those entities-- MEASFT also displays message 2000, indicating that that output file was not created. If the data file contained no data for any of the entities to be included in any output data file, MEASFT also displays message 2006, indicating that no output data files were created. RecoveryRecovery Determine the reason for the missing data and decide whether this is a significant issue. ## MEASFT 2008 (WARN_REMAINING_ENTITY_HAS_NO_DATA)MEASFT 2008 (WARN_REMAINING_ENTITY_HAS_NO_DATA) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause No data records were encountered for the last output file, whose entity-list was specified as a wildcard (*) in the SPLIT command. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. The specified output file is not created. RecoveryRecovery Determine the reason for the missing data and decide whether this is a significant issue. ## MEASFT 2009 (WARN_CPU_HAS_NO_DATA)MEASFT 2009 (WARN_CPU_HAS_NO_DATA) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause No data records were encountered for CPU nn , which was specified in the SPLIT command split criteria. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. If the data file contained no data records for any CPU to be represented in an output file, message 2000 is also displayed. RecoveryRecovery Determine the reason for the missing data and decide whether this is a significant issue. ``` No output files were created, no records satisfied split criteria. ``` ``` No data for this specified entity, entity ``` ``` Output file for the remaining entities (*) was not created due to lack of data. ``` ``` No data for this specified CPU, nn ``` ## MEASFT 2010 (WARN_REMAINING_CPU_HAS_NO_DATA)MEASFT 2010 (WARN_REMAINING_CPU_HAS_NO_DATA) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause No data records were encountered for the last output file, whose cpu-list was specified as a wildcard (*) in the SPLIT command. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. The specified output file is not created. RecoveryRecovery Determine the reason for the missing data and decide whether this is a significant issue. ## MEASFT 2011 (WARN_SPLIT_FILE_NOT_CREATED_2)MEASFT 2011 (WARN_SPLIT_FILE_NOT_CREATED_2) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause An output file name was not created because MEASFT found no data records that satisfied the split criteria for that file. This warning is always preceded by message 2007 and occurs only when an asterisk or a prefix followed by an asterisk was specified in the output file set. EffectEffect The SPLIT command continues. The specified output file is not created. RecoveryRecovery Remove the split criteria for the output file associated with this message. See the preceding occurrence of message 2007 to know which split criterion caused the problem. ## MEASFT 2032 (WARN_MISSING_JOURNAL)MEASFT 2032 (WARN_MISSING_JOURNAL) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Expected journal was not encountered; journal-type can be either OSS or SQL. EffectEffect The command continues. RecoveryRecovery Check the file size of the Measure data file to see whether the file was truncated; truncation can sometimes arise when you transfer a very large file. If the file was not truncated, it might be corrupted. If neither of these cases seems to apply, report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 2033 (WARN_READ_INVALID_DATA)MEASFT 2033 (WARN_READ_INVALID_DATA) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A Measure data file has an invalid record. The record could be invalid because the disk where the data file was collected was full or because the MEASCOM SWAP volume was full. EffectEffect The command continues by marking the invalid record as the EOF of the Measure data file. The rest of the records in the data file are ``` Output file for the remaining CPUs (*) was not created due to lack of data. ``` ``` An output file was not created due to lack of data. ``` ``` journal-type Journal missing. ``` ``` An invalid record was found in Measure data file ``` ignored. RecoveryRecovery Check the size of the disk where the Measure data file was collected or check the size of the MEASCOM SWAP volume on the measurement system. If these are full, either relocate the file to other disks or erase files to create more free space, and then re-run the measurement. ## MEASFT 3000 (ERR_FNAME_RESOLVE)MEASFT 3000 (ERR_FNAME_RESOLVE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A Measure data file name was specified that could not be resolved by FILENAME_RESOLVE_. EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the specified Measure data file name could not be resolved. Bad file name syntax is the most likely cause. ## MEASFT 3001 (ERR_FNAME_UNRESOLVE)MEASFT 3001 (ERR_FNAME_UNRESOLVE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A Measure data file name was specified that could not be unresolved by FILENAME_UNRESOLVE_. EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the specified Measure data file name could not be unresolved. This error is unlikely to occur because the file name has already been resolved by FILENAME_RESOLVE_. ## MEASFT 3002 (ERR_OPEN_MEASFILE)MEASFT 3002 (ERR_OPEN_MEASFILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A Measure data file name was specified that could not be opened. EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the specified Measure data file could not be opened. Likely reasons are the data file is missing, in use by another user, or not secured for read access for the user. ## MEASFT 3005 (ERR_CREATE_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3005 (ERR_CREATE_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` Measfile Name Resolution failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Measfile Name UnResolution failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Measfile Open failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Creation of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` The output file, datafile, could not be created. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the specified output file could not be created. Likely reasons are that the volume name was misspelled, the subvolume is not secured for write access for the user, or the volume is full. ## MEASFT 3006 (ERR_OPEN_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3006 (ERR_OPEN_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The output file, datafile, could not be opened. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the specified output file could not be opened. This error is unlikely to occur because the output file has just been created successfully. ## MEASFT 3007 (ERR_FILE_SETPOSITION)MEASFT 3007 (ERR_FILE_SETPOSITION) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Measure data file could not be repositioned at the beginning of the file or near the end of the file if the data file has a journal section. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the Measure data file could not be repositioned. ## MEASFT 3008 (ERR_WRITEX_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3008 (ERR_WRITEX_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an error writing to the output file, datafile. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. The created output files will be incomplete. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3009 (ERR_CLOSE_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3009 (ERR_CLOSE_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` Open of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` FILE_SETPOSITION_ failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Write of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Close of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` MEASFT encountered an error in closing the output file, datafile. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. The created output files will probably be incomplete. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3010 (ERR_FILE_SETPOS_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3010 (ERR_FILE_SETPOS_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an error in repositioning the output file, datafile, to the beginning of the file in order to update the journal pointer(s) in the first record. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. The created output files will not have correct journal pointers. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3011 (ERR_READX_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3011 (ERR_READX_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an error in reading the first record of the output file, datafile, so that it could update the journal pointer(s) in it. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. The created output files will not have correct journal pointers. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3012 (ERR_WRUPDX_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3012 (ERR_WRUPDX_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an error in writing back the first record of the output file, datafile, so that it could update the journal pointer(s) in it. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. The created output files will probably not have correct journal pointers. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3013 (ERR_READX_MEASFILE)MEASFT 3013 (ERR_READX_MEASFILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` FILE_SETPOSITION_ of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Read of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Writeupdate of output file, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Measfile READX failed, File System Error = # nn ``` MEASFT encountered an error in reading the journal records of the Measure data file. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. The created output files will not be complete. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3014 (ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND)MEASFT 3014 (ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not find the Measure data file. EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter the correct location of the Measure data file. ## MEASFT 3016 (ERR_FILE_GETINFO)MEASFT 3016 (ERR_FILE_GETINFO) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not get info on the specified data file. EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3017 (ERR_FILE_BAD_TYPE)MEASFT 3017 (ERR_FILE_BAD_TYPE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The specified Measure data file is not unstructured. EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Measure data files are unstructured files, whereas the one specified has file type n. Enter the correct file name. ## MEASFT 3018 (ERR_FILE_BAD_FILECODE)MEASFT 3018 (ERR_FILE_BAD_FILECODE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The specified Measure data file does not have a 175 file code. ``` Measure data file not found. ``` ``` FILE_GETINFOLISTBYNAME_ of datafile failed, File System Error = # nn. ``` ``` Measure file not unstructured, file type encountered # n ``` ``` Measure file code not 175, file code encountered # n ``` EffectEffect The SPLIT or INFO command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Measure data files have a file code of 175, whereas the one specified has file code n. Enter the correct file name. ## MEASFT 3019 (ERR_SPLIT_FILE_NAME_ERR)MEASFT 3019 (ERR_SPLIT_FILE_NAME_ERR) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The output file name specified (either directly or indirectly through a wildcard) is the same as the input data file, the file that is to be split. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Re-specify an output file name that doesn't conflict with the input data file name. ## MEASFT 3020 (ERR_CREATE_SPLIT_FILE_DUP)MEASFT 3020 (ERR_CREATE_SPLIT_FILE_DUP) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The output file name specified (either directly or indirectly through a wildcard) already exists and it isn't a Measure data file. MEASFT will not overwrite a file that it is not an existing Measure data file. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Re-specify an output file name that doesn't conflict with existing file names. ## MEASFT 3021 (ERR_PURGE_SPLIT_FILE)MEASFT 3021 (ERR_PURGE_SPLIT_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The output file, datafile, could not be purged. MEASFT overwrites existing Measure data files if an output file with the same name is specified. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine why the specified output file could not be purged. ## MEASFT 3022 (ERR_SEG_ALLOCATE_)MEASFT 3022 (ERR_SEG_ALLOCATE_) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not allocate enough memory to run. ``` Specified output filename, outfile , is the same as the file to be split. ``` ``` Specified output filename, outfile , already exists and is not a measure data file. ``` ``` Purging output filename, datafile , failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` SEGMENT_ALLOCATE_ error # nn. ``` EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the SEGMENT_ALLOCATE_ error code, nn, to determine why memory could not be allocated. MEASFT requires about 3MB of memory to run. ## MEASFT 3023 (ERR_USESEGMENT)MEASFT 3023 (ERR_USESEGMENT) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not access enough memory to run. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3024 (ERR_CRL_BAD_REC_TYPE)MEASFT 3024 (ERR_CRL_BAD_REC_TYPE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an unexpected record type. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files might have been created. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative, citing the record type, mm, and context, nn, displayed in this message. ## MEASFT 3025 (ERR_CONTAB_TRLR_WRONG_CTXT)MEASFT 3025 (ERR_CONTAB_TRLR_WRONG_CTXT) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered a record type it did not expect. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3027 (ERR_BAD_RECORD_STATE)MEASFT 3027 (ERR_BAD_RECORD_STATE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` USESEGMENT error. ``` ``` Bad record type # mm in this context # nn ``` ``` Unexpected CONTAB^TRAILER record in MF_NORMAL context. ``` ``` Bad record state in record # number ``` MEASFT encountered a record state it did not expect in record number. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3028 (ERR_BAD_DFFIRST_REC)MEASFT 3028 (ERR_BAD_DFFIRST_REC) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT did not recognize the first record in the Measure data file, whose version was Xnn. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3029 (ERR_UNEXPECTED_NONCTR_REC)MEASFT 3029 (ERR_UNEXPECTED_NONCTR_REC) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered a record type it did not expect in record number. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files might have been created. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3030 (ERR_UNSUPPORTED_VERSION)MEASFT 3030 (ERR_UNSUPPORTED_VERSION) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered a Measure data file version created by the Xnn version of Measure. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error RecoveryRecovery MEASFT does not support data files created by Measure versions earlier than G10. ## MEASFT 3031 (ERR_NO_DFFIRST_REC)MEASFT 3031 (ERR_NO_DFFIRST_REC) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` Measure data file corrupt, 1st record wrong length, file version Xnn ``` ``` Unexpected non-counter record # number ``` ``` Unsupported Measure data file version, Xnn ``` ``` Measure data file corrupt, 1st record not present. ``` CauseCause MEASFT did not recognize the first record in the Measure data file. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3033 (ERR_REC_LENGTH_COMP_ERR)MEASFT 3033 (ERR_REC_LENGTH_COMP_ERR) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not compute the length of record number nnnnn because of either a MEASFT defect or a corrupt Measure data file. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery If the Measure data file seems correct, contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3034 (ERR_REC_LENGTH_TOO_BIG)MEASFT 3034 (ERR_REC_LENGTH_TOO_BIG) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The length of record number nnnnn is too big. This error is caused by either a MEASFT defect or a corrupt Measure data file. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files may have been created. RecoveryRecovery If the Measure data file seems correct, contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3035 (ERR_BAD_RECORD_TYPE)MEASFT 3035 (ERR_BAD_RECORD_TYPE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an invalid record type in record number nnnnn. The cause may be a corrupt Measure data file or the specified input data file is not a Measure data file. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files might have been created. RecoveryRecovery Verify that the specified Measure data file is correct. ## MEASFT 3036 (ERR_READ_NO_DATA_ERR)MEASFT 3036 (ERR_READ_NO_DATA_ERR) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` Record length could not be computed, record # nnnnn ``` ``` Record length too big, record # nnnnn ``` ``` Invalid record type, record # nnnnn ``` ``` File error, read no data, File System Error = # nn , record # nnnnn ``` CauseCause MEASFT read no data and got File System error nn, at record number nnnnn. The cause may be a corrupt Measure data file or the specified input data file is not a Measure data file. EffectEffect The command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. Some output files might have been created. RecoveryRecovery Verify that the specified Measure data file is correct. ## MEASFT 3038 (ERR_NO_XRTIMES_RECORD)MEASFT 3038 (ERR_NO_XRTIMES_RECORD) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME command was issued, but there is no Start Measurement record in the input data file. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3039 (ERR_BAD_XRTIMES_TIMESTAMP)MEASFT 3039 (ERR_BAD_XRTIMES_TIMESTAMP) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME command was issued, but the Start Measurement record has a bad timestamp. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Either the Measure data file has been corrupted or MEASFT has a defect. Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 3040 (ERR_COMPUTETIMESTAMP)MEASFT 3040 (ERR_COMPUTETIMESTAMP) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME command was issued, but the timestamp in the Start Measurement record caused a COMPUTETIMESTAMP error. This is an internal MEASFT error. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative, citing the mask, mm, displayed in the message. ## MEASFT 3041 (ERR_SEG_ALLOCATE64)MEASFT 3041 (ERR_SEG_ALLOCATE64) ``` No Start Measurement record in the input data file. ``` ``` Bad Start Measurement record in the input data file. ``` ``` COMPUTETIMESTAMP error, mask = # mm. ``` ``` SEGMENT_ALLOCATE64_ error #nn, size #ss ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not allocate enough 64-bit memory. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the SEGMENT_ALLOCATE64_ error code, #nn, and the size, #ss, to determine why memory could not be allocated. ## MEASFT 3042 (ERR_SEG_RESIZE64)MEASFT 3042 (ERR_SEG_RESIZE64) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT could not resize enough 64-bit memory. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the SEGMENT_RESIZE64_ error code, #nn, and the new size, #news, and the oldsize, #olds, to determine why memory could not be resized. ## MEASFT 3043 (ERR_SIG_HANDLER)MEASFT 3043 (ERR_SIG_HANDLER) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A signal was caught. EffectEffect Open output files are purged, open files are closed and MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery For more information about the signal, see HPE Nonstop Open System Services Programmer’s Guide. ## MEASFT 3200 (ERR_PARSE_NOT_KEYWORD)MEASFT 3200 (ERR_PARSE_NOT_KEYWORD) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The string, XXX, in the command line is not a valid keyword. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter the correct keyword or command syntax. ## MEASFT 3201 (ERR_PARSE_NO_FILENAME)MEASFT 3201 (ERR_PARSE_NO_FILENAME) ``` SEGMENT_RESIZE64_ error #nn, resize #news, oldsize #olds ``` ``` Signal ``` ``` XXX is not a recognized keyword. ``` ``` filename parameter is missing. ``` ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Measure data file was not specified in the command line. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter the Measure data file name. ## MEASFT 3202 (ERR_PARSE_FILENAME_TOO_LONG)MEASFT 3202 (ERR_PARSE_FILENAME_TOO_LONG) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Measure data file name entered in the command line is too long. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter the correct Measure data file name ## MEASFT 3230 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_NAME_TOO_LONG)MEASFT 3230 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_NAME_TOO_LONG) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The indicated output file name is too long. This error occurs when an unqualified output file name greater than 8 bytes is entered. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter the correct output file name. ## MEASFT 3231 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_PREFIX_TOO_LONG)MEASFT 3231 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_PREFIX_TOO_LONG) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The indicated output file name prefix is too long. Maximum prefix length is 6 bytes. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter a shorter output file prefix. ## MEASFT 3232 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_FNAME_RESOLVE)MEASFT 3232 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_FNAME_RESOLVE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` filename parameter too long. ``` ``` SPLIT name parameter too long. ``` ``` SPLIT name-prefix parameter too long. ``` ``` SPLIT name Resolution failed, File System Error = # nn ``` CauseCause The output file name specified could not be resolved by FILENAME_RESOLVE_. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. ## MEASFT 3233 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_FNAME_UNRESOLVE)MEASFT 3233 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_FNAME_UNRESOLVE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The output file name specified could not be unresolved by FILENAME_UNRESOLVE_. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Use the displayed File System error number, nn, to determine what caused the error. This error is unlikely to occur because the output file name has already been resolved by FILENAME_RESOLVE_. ## MEASFT 3234 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_RPAREN_MISSING)MEASFT 3234 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_RPAREN_MISSING) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT file specifications entry starts with a left parenthesis and is missing a right parenthesis. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT file specifications syntax. ## MEASFT 3235 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BY_EXPECTED)MEASFT 3235 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BY_EXPECTED) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT split criteria entry must start with "BY". EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT split criteria syntax. ## MEASFT 3236 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BY_KW_EXPECTED)MEASFT 3236 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BY_KW_EXPECTED) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` SPLIT name UnResolution failed, File System Error = # nn ``` ``` SPLIT file specs right parenthesis missing. ``` ``` SPLIT 'BY' keyword missing. ``` ``` SPLIT 'BY CPU' or 'BY ENTITY' expected. ``` CauseCause The SPLIT split criteria entry must start with "BY CPU" or "BY ENTITY". EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT split criteria syntax. ## MEASFT 3237 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_RPAREN_MISSING)MEASFT 3237 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_RPAREN_MISSING) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT split criteria entry starts with a left parenthesis and is missing a right parenthesis. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT split criteria syntax. ## MEASFT 3238 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_MISMATCH)MEASFT 3238 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_MISMATCH) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT file specifications and split criteria entries conflict in the number of output files specified. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT files specifications and/or split criteria syntax so that they specify the same number of output files. ## MEASFT 3239 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_CPU_NUM2)MEASFT 3239 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_CPU_NUM2) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The CPU specified in the SPLIT command split criteria is either out of range (0 to 15) or is not numeric. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the invalid CPU number. ## MEASFT 3240 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_CPU_WC_ERR1)MEASFT 3240 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_CPU_WC_ERR1) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` SPLIT split criteria right parenthesis missing. ``` ``` SPLIT file specs and split criteria mismatch. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria CPU number out of range. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria CPU '*' must be in last cpu-list. ``` The wildcard (*) specified in the SPLIT command split criteria was not in the last cpu-list. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY CPU entry so that the wildcard cpu-list is the last entry in the list. ## MEASFT 3241 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_CPU_WC_ERR2)MEASFT 3241 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_CPU_WC_ERR2) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The wildcard (*) specified in the SPLIT command split criteria was not the only item in the last cpu-list. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY CPU entry so that the wildcard cpu-list is the last list and the wildcard is the only entry in that list. ## MEASFT 3242 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_WC_ERR1)MEASFT 3242 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_WC_ERR1) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The wildcard (*) specified in the SPLIT command split criteria cannot be prepended to anything; it must be the last entry in the command line when no lists are specified. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT split criteria so that the wildcard is the last element on the command line. ## MEASFT 3243 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_WC_ERR2)MEASFT 3243 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_WC_ERR2) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause If wildcard (*) is specified in the SPLIT command split criteria, then the file set must also be a wildcard. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT split criteria so that both the file specifications and split criteria have wildcards or neither has a wildcard. ## MEASFT 3244 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_EMPTY)MEASFT 3244 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_EMPTY) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause ``` SPLIT criteria CPU '*' must be only item in last cpu-list. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria '*' can not be prepended to anything. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria '*' requires that out-file-set be a '*'. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria empty. ``` Nothing was specified between the parentheses in the SPLIT command split criteria. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter the desired SPLIT split criteria. ## MEASFT 3245 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_ENT_WC_ERR1)MEASFT 3245 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_ENT_WC_ERR1) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The wildcard (*) specified in the SPLIT command split criteria was not in the last entity-list. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY ENTITY entry so that the wildcard entity-list is the last one. ## MEASFT 3246 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_ENT_WC_ERR2)MEASFT 3246 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_ENT_WC_ERR2) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The wildcard (*) specified in the SPLIT command split criteria was not the only item in the last entity-list. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY ENTITY entry so that the wildcard entity-list is the last list and the wildcard is the only entry in that list. ## MEASFT 3247 (ERR_PARSE_NOT_ENT_KEYWORD)MEASFT 3247 (ERR_PARSE_NOT_ENT_KEYWORD) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The string, XXX, in the command line is not a valid entity name. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Enter a correct entity name. ## MEASFT 3248 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_ONE_FILE)MEASFT 3248 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_ONE_FILE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Only one output file name was specified but no split criterion was specified. ``` SPLIT criteria Entity '*' must be in last entity-list. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria Entity '*' must be only item in last entity-list. ``` ``` XXX is not a recognized Entity. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria required (no wildcard) for a single output file. ``` EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Add split criteria to the specified SPLIT command. ## MEASFT 3249 (ERR_PARSE_FS_SYNTAX)MEASFT 3249 (ERR_PARSE_FS_SYNTAX) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Multiple output file names were specified in the file specifications with no parentheses. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT file specifications syntax. ## MEASFT 3250 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_FS_WC_ERR)MEASFT 3250 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_FS_WC_ERR) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Entries in the SPLIT command file specifications cannot use a wildcard if names are enclosed in parentheses. In fact, the wildcard is only permitted by itself or as a output file name prefix. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT file specifications syntax. ## MEASFT 3251 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_CPU_NUM1)MEASFT 3251 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_CPU_NUM1) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The CPU specified in the SPLIT command split criteria is not numeric or is out of range. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the CPU number in error. ## MEASFT 3252 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_BAD)MEASFT 3252 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_SC_BAD) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Something other than a wildcard or left parenthesis followed the "BY CPU" or "BY ENTITY" phrase. ``` SPLIT out-file-set syntax error. ``` ``` SPLIT out-file-set filename in list cannot have an '*'. ``` ``` SPLIT criteria CPU not numeric or out of range. ``` ``` Syntax error after 'BY CPU/ENTITY', expected '*' or '('. ``` EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT split criteria syntax. ## MEASFT 3253 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_MISSING_DEL)MEASFT 3253 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_MISSING_DEL) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause A delimiter (either a comma or a right parenthesis) is missing in the SPLIT file specifications. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT file specifications syntax. ## MEASFT 3254 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_TIME_KW_EXP)MEASFT 3254 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_TIME_KW_EXP) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME command must be followed by the FROM, TO, FOR or INTO keyword. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME syntax. ## MEASFT 3255 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_FROM_TIME)MEASFT 3255 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_FROM_TIME) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME FROM time value is not valid. The time syntax is: At least one space must separate the day, month, and year items. Also, the items must have valid values. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME FROM syntax. ## MEASFT 3256 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_TO_TIME)MEASFT 3256 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_TO_TIME) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation ``` SPLIT file specs missing a delimiter (',' or ')'). ``` ``` SPLIT 'FROM', 'TO', 'FOR' or INTO keyword expected. ``` ``` SPLIT FROM time value not valid. ``` ``` [ day month year , ] hr : min [ : sec ] ``` ``` SPLIT TO time value not valid. ``` CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME TO time value is not valid. The time syntax is: At least one space must separate the day, month, and year items. Also, the items must have valid values. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME TO syntax. ## MEASFT 3257 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_TO_FROM_TIME)MEASFT 3257 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_TO_FROM_TIME) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME TO time value must be greater than the FROM time value. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME FROM and/or TO time values. ## MEASFT 3258 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_FOR_TIME)MEASFT 3258 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_FOR_TIME) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME FOR duration value is not valid. The duration syntax is: where number can be 1 to 32767 and it must be followed by SECOND, MINUTE, or HOUR. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME FOR syntax. ## MEASFT 3259 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_INTO_OPT)MEASFT 3259 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_INTO_OPT) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME INTO syntax is not valid. The INTO syntax is: where number can be 1 to 32767 if followed by SECOND, MINUTE, or HOUR, or 2 to 100 if followed by no value or PARTS. EffectEffect ``` [ day month year , ] hr : min [ : sec ] ``` ``` SPLIT TO time value not greater than FROM time value. ``` ``` SPLIT FOR time value not valid. ``` ``` number {SECOND[S] | MINUTES[S] | HOUR[S] } ``` ``` SPLIT INTO option value not valid. ``` ``` number [ SECOND | MINUTE | HOUR ][ PARTS ] ``` The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME INTO syntax. ## MEASFT 3260 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_INTO_VAL)MEASFT 3260 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_BAD_INTO_VAL) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The SPLIT BY TIME INTO number value for PARTS is out of range. The valid range is 2 to 100. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME INTO number value. ## MEASFT 3261 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_TIME_FROM_KW_EXP)MEASFT 3261 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_TIME_FROM_KW_EXP) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The keyword following the SPLIT BY TIME FROM option is not TO or FOR. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME FROM syntax. ## MEASFT 3262 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_TIME_WC)MEASFT 3262 (ERR_PARSE_SPLIT_TIME_WC) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The wildcard is not allowed in the SPLIT BY TIME command. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Correct the SPLIT BY TIME syntax. ## MEASFT 3300 (ERR_CANNOT_SPLIT)MEASFT 3300 (ERR_CANNOT_SPLIT) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Measure data file contains data records for only one entity, so it cannot be split by entity. This error occurs only when no split criterion is specified; by default, MEASFT attempts to split the data file by entity. CauseCause ``` SPLIT INTO option value range error, max PARTS is 100. ``` ``` SPLIT 'TO' or 'FOR' keyword expected. ``` ``` SPLIT, BY TIME * command not allowed. ``` ``` The specified measure file cannot be split by entity, try splitting by CPU. ``` The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Try splitting by CPU. ## MEASFT 3301 (ERR_CANNOT_SPLIT_BY_TIME)MEASFT 3301 (ERR_CANNOT_SPLIT_BY_TIME) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause The Measure data file is a pre-H01 data file. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery None. ## MEASFT 4000 (ERR_PROCHAND_GETMINE)MEASFT 4000 (ERR_PROCHAND_GETMINE) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered error nn in PROCESSHANDLE_GETMINE_ EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact Hewlett Packard Enterprise. ## MEASFT 4001 (ERR_PROCHAND_DECOMP)MEASFT 4001 (ERR_PROCHAND_DECOMP) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered File System error nn in PROCESSHANDLE_DECOMPOSE_ EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact Hewlett Packard Enterprise. ## MEASFT 4002 (ERR_NOMEMORY_RECBUF)MEASFT 4002 (ERR_NOMEMORY_RECBUF) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT ran out of memory and reported nnnn bytes left. This condition is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT abends. ``` Only H01 and later data files can be split by TIME, file version VNN. ``` ``` PROCESSHANDLE_GETMINE_ Error = # nn ``` ``` PROCESSHANDLE_DECOMPOSE_ File System Error = # nn ``` ``` Not enough memory, bytes remaining = nnnn ``` RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4003 (ERR_NOMEMORY_SPLITBUF)MEASFT 4003 (ERR_NOMEMORY_SPLITBUF) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT ran out of memory and reported nnnn bytes left. This condition is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4004 (ERR_NUM_SPFILES_ERR1)MEASFT 4004 (ERR_NUM_SPFILES_ERR1) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an internal error with the internal Num_spfiles variable whose value is reported as nn. The text is the same as that for error 4005 (ERR_NUM_SPFILES_ERR2), but the underlying error differs. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4005 (ERR_NUM_SPFILES_ERR2)MEASFT 4005 (ERR_NUM_SPFILES_ERR2) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an internal error with the internal Num_spfiles variable whose value is reported as nn. The text is the same as that for error 4004 (ERR_NUM_SPFILES_ERR1), but the underlying error differs. EffectEffect The SPLIT command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4007 (ERR_BAD_SPF_EOF)MEASFT 4007 (ERR_BAD_SPF_EOF) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT encountered an internal error and miscalculated the output file EOF. It expected nnnnnn, which is a number consisting of five to ten decimal digits. EffectEffect ``` Not enough memory for output buffer, bytes remaining = nnnn ``` ``` Internal error with variable, Num_spfiles = nn ``` ``` Internal error with variable, Num_spfiles = nn ``` ``` Output file EOF bad, expected = nnnnnn ``` The command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4010 (ERR_BAD_MF_CONTEXT)MEASFT 4010 (ERR_BAD_MF_CONTEXT) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT internal logic error. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4012 (ERR_PARSE_KW)MEASFT 4012 (ERR_PARSE_KW) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT internal logic error in parsing the command line. This condition is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4013 (ERR_DIDNT_GET_INFO_DATA)MEASFT 4013 (ERR_DIDNT_GET_INFO_DATA) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT internal logic error in the INFO or SPLIT command. This condition is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4014 (ERR_RB_STATE_ERROR)MEASFT 4014 (ERR_RB_STATE_ERROR) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT internal logic error in the INFO or SPLIT command occurred at record number nnnnn. This condition is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT abends. ``` Bad Measfile_context # nn ``` ``` Internal error, bad keyword index = # nn ``` ``` Internal error, didn't get the INFO data. ``` ``` Bad record buffer state, record # nnnnn ``` RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4015 (ERR_BAD_JOURNAL_SELECTION)MEASFT 4015 (ERR_BAD_JOURNAL_SELECTION) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause MEASFT internal logic error in the INFO or SPLIT command. This condition is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The command is not executed. MEASFT abends. RecoveryRecovery Contact your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative. ## MEASFT 4016 (ERR_NOMEMORY_TIME128)MEASFT 4016 (ERR_NOMEMORY_TIME128) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Internal error in memory allocation. This is a MEASFT defect. EffectEffect The SPLIT command does not complete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Report this message to your Hewlett Packard Enterprise representative, citing the number, nn, displayed in the message. ## MEASFT 4018 (ERR_TIME128_ENTRY_NOT_FOUND)MEASFT 4018 (ERR_TIME128_ENTRY_NOT_FOUND) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause Number of entity records exceeded Maximum TIME128 entries. EffectEffect The SPLIT file is incomplete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery Do a CPU or ENTITY split first, then do a TIME split on the split files. ## MEASFT 4019 (ERR_TIME128_ARRAY_FULL)MEASFT 4019 (ERR_TIME128_ARRAY_FULL) ## Message ExplanationMessage Explanation CauseCause TIME128 entry is not found in TIME128 array and array is full. EffectEffect The SPLIT File is incomplete. MEASFT terminates in error. RecoveryRecovery ``` Internal error, bad Journal selection = # n ``` ``` Not enough memory for TIME128 array, bytes remaining = # nn. ``` ``` TIME128 entry is not found in TIME128 array. ``` ``` TIME128 array is full. ``` Do a CPU or ENTITY split first, then do a TIME split on the split files.
